locked_parameters.observable_distribution_P: the randomized binary-treatment law of (D,S,SY), where D in {0,1}, S is the realized post-treatment selection/collider indicator, and Y in {0,1} is observed only when S=1.
locked_parameters.parameter_theta: E[Y(1)-Y(0) | S(1)=S(0)=1], the Lee always-selected average treatment effect for the outcome scale on which Y is defined.
locked_parameters.identifying_restrictions: random assignment and consistency; binary principal selection strata A=(1,1), G=(0,1), H=(1,0), N=(0,0); a finite reverse-selection budget kappa limiting the harmed stratum H; and an arm-specific collider odds-ratio bound Gamma on the association, inside each selected arm, between the always-selected stratum A and the selected outcome Y(d).
locked_parameters.bounding_functional: the alpha-profile quadratic selected-mixture envelope B_{Gamma,kappa}(P), obtained by varying the always-selected share alpha=P(A)>0 over its positive observable/reverse-budget interval and solving the two arm-specific odds-ratio saturation quadratics for the feasible always-selected means.

named_assumptions.label: ass:random-consistency
named_assumptions.statement: Treatment \(D\) is independent of \((S(0),S(1),Y(0),Y(1))\), both treatment arms have positive probability, and observed variables satisfy \(S=S(D)\) and \(Y=Y(D)\) whenever \(S=1\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:binary-support
named_assumptions.statement: For each \(d\in\{0,1\}\), \(S(d)\in\{0,1\}\) and \(Y(d)\in\{0,1\}\). The observed selected rates and selected means satisfy \(p_d\in(0,1)\) and \(m_d\in(0,1)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:reverse-budget
named_assumptions.statement: For a fixed \(\kappa\in[0,\infty]\), the harmed principal stratum mass \(h=P(S(0)=1,S(1)=0)\) satisfies \(h\le \kappa p_0\). Equivalently, admissible always-selected shares must lie in \(\mathcal A_\kappa(P)\), and the target profile is restricted to \(\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:gamma-mixture
named_assumptions.statement: For a fixed \(\Gamma\in[1,\infty]\), each selected arm \(d\in\{0,1\}\) obeys \(OR_d(t_d;\alpha)\in[\Gamma^{-1},\Gamma]\) for its selected-arm \(2\times2\) table with margins \(r_d(\alpha)\) and \(m_d\). When \(\Gamma=\infty\), this restriction is interpreted as the vacuous Frechet restriction \(t_d\in[L_d(\alpha),U_d(\alpha)]\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:interior
named_assumptions.statement: The selected mixture is in the finite interior regime \(\Gamma\in(1,\infty)\). The positive alpha profile is a nonempty compact interval \(\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)=[\alpha_-,\alpha_+]\) with \(0<\alpha_-\le \alpha_+\le \min\{p_0,p_1\}\), each selected-arm feasible set is nonempty, and \(r_d(\alpha)\in(0,1)\) for both arms and every \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:exposed-endpoints
named_assumptions.statement: For each endpoint direction \(\ell\in\{-,+\}\) used in the comparison theorem, the SGJR/JR-induced beta/psi comparator optimizer set and the Honore--Hu-style no-exclusion endpoint set are nonempty; strict separation conclusions additionally assume local uniqueness and strict exposure.

estimand_functional: B_{\Gamma,\kappa}(P)=[\theta_L(P;\Gamma,\kappa),\theta_U(P;\Gamma,\kappa)] with \theta_L=\min_{\alpha\in\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)}\{\underline a_1(\alpha)-\overline a_0(\alpha)\} and \theta_U=\max_{\alpha\in\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)}\{\overline a_1(\alpha)-\underline a_0(\alpha)\}.

conjectures.label: lee-gamma-frontier
conjectures.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:random-consistency}--\ref{ass:interior}, the sharp identified set for the always-selected effect is \[ \Theta_I(P;\Gamma,\kappa)= \left[ \inf_{\alpha\in\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)} \{\underline a_1(\alpha)-\overline a_0(\alpha)\}, \sup_{\alpha\in\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)} \{\overline a_1(\alpha)-\underline a_0(\alpha)\} \right], \] where \(\underline a_d(\alpha)\) and \(\overline a_d(\alpha)\) are the armwise quadratic endpoints from Theorem~\ref{thm:arm-quadratic}. Moreover, each global endpoint is attained by an explicit four-stratum law \((A,G,H,N)\) whose two selected-arm \(2\times2\) tables saturate the appropriate odds-ratio boundary, and the optimizing \(\alpha\) is either an endpoint of \(\mathcal A_\kappa^+(P)\) or a root of the derivative of a quadratic-radical profile induced by the active odds-ratio equations. For an open set of observable laws satisfying the comparison regime in Assumption~\ref{ass:exposed-endpoints}, this frontier is conjectured not to be representable as either the \citet{HuberMellace2015SharpSelection} monotonicity/stochastic-dominance sharp-bound geometry, a \citet{LaffersNedela2017Sensitivity} Lee sensitivity program with only monotonicity/exogeneity relaxation parameters, a \citet{Semenova2025GeneralizedLee} conditional-monotonicity Lee bound, the stochastic-monotonicity-plus-symmetry geometry of \citet{Okamoto2025RobustifyLee}, or a margin-free application of the \citet{ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006}, \citet{JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007}, \citet{ShepherdRedmanAnkerst2008}, and \citet{ShepherdGilbertDupont2011} beta/psi principal-stratum sensitivity family. The statement explicitly concedes that beta \(=\log OR_d(t_d;r_d(\alpha),m_d)\) reproduces each armwise selected table; the conjectured non-equivalence is the sharper claim that without adding the same \(\alpha\)-coupling and \(H\le \kappa p_0\) restriction, those armwise beta tables do not define the same global sharp ATE endpoint. The distinguishing certificate is an interior active odds-ratio equality \(OR_d(t_d;r_d(\alpha),m_d)\in\{\Gamma^{-1},\Gamma\}\) plus the cross-arm equalities \(p_1r_1(\alpha)=p_0r_0(\alpha)=\alpha\), with no tail-trimming quantile cutoff, stochastic-dominance ordering, stochastic monotonicity/symmetry condition, covariate sign partition, or right-censoring survival model that pins the same coupled selected-cell endpoint. A concrete open-cell witness target is \(p_1=0.6\), \(p_0=0.5\), \(m_1=0.5\), \(m_0=0.4\), \(\Gamma=4\), \(\kappa=0.5\), and \(\alpha=0.3\): both \(G=0.3\) and \(H=0.2\) are positive. At this witness the local upper ATE endpoint uses \(t_1=1/3\) and \(t_0=(-1+\sqrt{3.88})/6\), while the local lower ATE endpoint uses \(t_1=1/6\) and \(t_0=(4-\sqrt{4.48})/6\); the corresponding armwise beta values are \(\pm\log 4\), so the certificate is not the absence of a log-odds parametrization but the extra \((\alpha,\kappa)\) compatibility restriction: \[\begin{array}{c|cc|c|c} \text{arm/endpoint} & P(A,Y=1\mid S(d)=1) & P(A,Y=0\mid S(d)=1) & P(A^c,Y=1\mid S(d)=1) & P(A^c,Y=0\mid S(d)=1)\\ \hline d=1,\ \text{upper} & 0.333 & 0.167 & 0.167 & 0.333\\ d=1,\ \text{lower} & 0.167 & 0.333 & 0.333 & 0.167\\ d=0,\ \text{upper for }a_0 & 0.314 & 0.286 & 0.086 & 0.314\\ d=0,\ \text{lower for }a_0 & 0.162 & 0.438 & 0.238 & 0.162 \end{array}\] Thus the displayed witness gives local ATE values approximately \([-0.190,0.397]\) at \(\alpha=0.3\), with all active cells interior rather than tail-trimming faces.

conjectures.label: strict-tightening
conjectures.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:random-consistency}--\ref{ass:exposed-endpoints}, if \(\Gamma<\infty\) and every unrestricted \(\Gamma=\infty\) optimizer for the lower or upper ATE endpoint uses at least one selected-arm cell on a nondegenerate Fréchet equality face \(t_d=L_d(\alpha)\) or \(t_d=U_d(\alpha)\) with \(0<L_d(\alpha)<U_d(\alpha)<1\), then the interval in Conjecture~\ref{conj:lee-gamma-frontier} is a strict subset of the unrestricted bidirectional-principal-strata interval obtained by setting \(\Gamma=\infty\). Within the interior assumptions, equality is possible only through profile-level compensation: a different positive \(\alpha\) must reproduce the unrestricted ATE endpoint while all active arm cells satisfy the finite-\(\Gamma\) odds-ratio inequalities. Outside the interior assumptions, equality can also occur when an optimizing \(\alpha\) forces a degenerate principal mixture \(r_d(\alpha)\in\{0,1\}\), an observed selected outcome margin has \(m_d\in\{0,1\}\), or the reverse-selection budget collapses to Lee monotonicity and the unrestricted endpoint is already compatible with the finite-\(\Gamma\) odds-ratio face.

conjectures.label: coupling-iff
conjectures.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:random-consistency}--\ref{ass:exposed-endpoints}, fix an endpoint direction \(\ell\in\{-,+\}\). Let \(b_\ell(P;\Gamma,\kappa)\) be the corresponding support value of the \((\Gamma,\kappa)\) frontier in Conjecture~\ref{conj:lee-gamma-frontier}. Then \(b_\ell(P;\Gamma,\kappa)=v_\ell^{\mathrm{SGJR}}(P;\Gamma)\) for the finite-binary \citet{ShepherdGilbertJemiaiRotnitzky2006} / \citet{JemiaiRotnitzkyShepherdGilbert2007}-induced beta/psi endpoint problem if and only if \(\Delta_\ell^{\mathrm{SGJR}}(P;\Gamma,\kappa)=0\). If \(\Delta_\ell^{\mathrm{SGJR}}(P;\Gamma,\kappa)>0\), then \(b_\ell(P;\Gamma,\kappa)\) is strictly inside that SGJR/JR-induced armwise beta/psi benchmark. Separately, \(b_\ell(P;\Gamma,\kappa)=v_\ell^{\mathrm{HH}}(P;\Gamma)\) for the proposal-defined binary no-exclusion support benchmark inspired by \citet{HonoreHu2020Selection} only if \(\Delta_\ell^{\mathrm{HH}}(P;\Gamma,\kappa)=0\); under local uniqueness and strict exposure, \(\Delta_\ell^{\mathrm{HH}}(P;\Gamma,\kappa)>0\) implies strict separation from the no-exclusion endpoint.

supporting_theorems.label: arm-quadratic
supporting_theorems.statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:random-consistency}, \ref{ass:binary-support}, and \ref{ass:gamma-mixture}, fix an arm \(d\) and an admissible \(\alpha\) with \(r=r_d(\alpha)\in(0,1)\) and \(m=m_d\in(0,1)\). The feasible values of \(t=P(A,Y(d)=1\mid S(d)=1)\) form a closed interval obtained by intersecting the Fréchet interval with the two odds-ratio quadratic inequalities \[ t(1-r-m+t)\le \Gamma(r-t)(m-t), \qquad \Gamma t(1-r-m+t)\ge (r-t)(m-t). \] Equivalently, the two possible active endpoint equations are quadratic polynomials in \(t\). The feasible always-selected mean in arm \(d\) is the corresponding interval \(a_d=t/r\).
supporting_theorems.proof_tex:
\begin{theorem}[Theorem 1: Armwise quadratic selected-mixture envelope]\label{thm:arm-quadratic}
Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:random-consistency}, \ref{ass:binary-support}, and \ref{ass:gamma-mixture}, fix an arm \(d\) and an admissible \(\alpha\) with \(r=r_d(\alpha)\in(0,1)\) and \(m=m_d\in(0,1)\).  The feasible values of \(t=P(A,Y(d)=1\mid S(d)=1)\) form a closed interval obtained by intersecting the Frechet interval with the two odds-ratio quadratic inequalities
\[
  t(1-r-m+t)\le \Gamma(r-t)(m-t),
  \qquad
  \Gamma t(1-r-m+t)\ge (r-t)(m-t).
\]
Equivalently, the two possible active endpoint equations are quadratic polynomials in \(t\).  The feasible always-selected mean in arm \(d\) is the corresponding interval \(a_d=t/r\).
\end{theorem}
\begin{proof}
By randomization and consistency, the selected-arm margins \(r=r_d(\alpha)=P(A\mid S(d)=1)\) and \(m=m_d=P(Y(d)=1\mid S(d)=1)\) are the margins of the selected-arm binary table with row variable \(A\) and column variable \(Y(d)=1\).  If \(t=P(A,Y(d)=1\mid S(d)=1)\), the four cell probabilities are
\[
  t,\qquad r-t,\qquad m-t,\qquad 1-r-m+t.
\]
Nonnegativity of these four cells is equivalent to the Frechet interval
\[
  \max\{0,r+m-1\}\le t\le \min\{r,m\}.
\]
This interval is closed.  On its interior, the selected-mixture odds ratio is
\[
  OR(t;r,m)=\frac{t(1-r-m+t)}{(r-t)(m-t)}.
\]
Because all cells are nonnegative on the Frechet interval, the bound \(OR(t;r,m)\le \Gamma\) is equivalent to
\[
  t(1-r-m+t)\le \Gamma(r-t)(m-t),
\]
and the bound \(OR(t;r,m)\ge \Gamma^{-1}\) is equivalent to
\[
  \Gamma t(1-r-m+t)\ge (r-t)(m-t).
\]
The same inequalities give the correct closed boundary convention when a numerator or denominator cell is zero.  Each displayed boundary equality is a quadratic polynomial in \(t\), since both sides are products of affine functions of \(t\).  The feasible \(t\)-set is therefore the intersection of one closed Frechet interval and two closed quadratic sublevel/superlevel sets, hence is closed.  It is an interval because, on the open Frechet interval, the derivative of the log odds ratio is
\[
  \frac{1}{t}+\frac{1}{1-r-m+t}+\frac{1}{r-t}+\frac{1}{m-t}>0,
\]
so \(OR(t;r,m)\) is strictly increasing from the lower Frechet boundary to the upper Frechet boundary.  Intersecting an interval with \([\Gamma^{-1},\Gamma]\) under a monotone continuous map gives a closed interval, possibly a singleton.  Finally, because \(r\in(0,1)\), division by \(r\) is order-preserving, and the feasible always-selected mean is exactly the image interval \(a_d=t/r\).
\end{proof}

substrate_survey.method: lean_local_search only; no Lean source files were opened or cited in the TeX artifact.
results: POManskiIVSystem, lowerBound1, BalkePearl, Lee, Manski, and PartialID did not surface directly reusable declarations in this project context.
POSystem surfaced AutoID.PO.POSystem and related counterfactual-independence primitives.
substrate_survey.bypass_justified: The Stage 0 setup is a finite binary selected-mixture table derivation with a new common-alpha compatibility map and armwise quadratic odds-ratio endpoints; available primitives are at adjacent or more generic abstraction levels.
